clusters.title: Georgia Special Election Heads to Runoff for Greene's Seat
clusters.summary: In Georgia's special election to fill the vacancy left by former Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene, Democrat Shawn Harris and Republican Clay Fuller have advanced to a runoff. Fuller, who received an endorsement from former President Donald Trump, will compete against Harris in a closely monitored race that could impact the political landscape in the region.

items.title: Georgia Race to Replace Marjorie Taylor Greene Heads to Runoff
items.description: Voters in Georgia held their special election to replace former Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene with Democrat Shawn Harris and Republican Clay Fuller, who was endorsed by President Donald Trump, advancing to the runoff in the closely-watched race. NBC’s Aaron Gilchrist reports for TODAY.

items.description: Clayton Fuller will face Shawn Harris, a Democrat, in an April runoff. Mr. Fuller’s win over a flashier Republican showed the power of the president’s support.

clusters.title: White House Denies Navy Escorting Tanker in Strait of Hormuz
clusters.summary: The White House has refuted claims that the U.S. Navy successfully escorted an oil tanker through the Strait of Hormuz, stating that no such escort has occurred. This statement comes in response to a now-deleted assertion that suggested otherwise, highlighting ongoing tensions in the region and the importance of maritime security. The clarification aims to address concerns regarding U.S. naval operations amid rising geopolitical tensions.

clusters.title: Three Cargo Ships Hit by Projectiles in Strait of Hormuz
clusters.summary: Three cargo ships were struck by projectiles in the Strait of Hormuz, leading to the reported disappearance of three crew members from a Thai vessel. The incident raises concerns about maritime security in a critical shipping lane, which is vital for global trade. Authorities are investigating the circumstances surrounding the attacks and their implications for regional stability.

clusters.title: Trump Demands Passage of SAVE America Act Before Signing Bills
clusters.summary: President Donald Trump has announced that he will not sign any legislation into law until the SAVE America Act is passed by Congress. This move intensifies his push for the voting bill ahead of the upcoming elections, highlighting the importance of electoral reforms in his agenda. The decision reflects Trump's ongoing efforts to influence legislative priorities within the Republican Party.

clusters.title: Gunfire Reported at U.S. Consulate in Toronto, Police Investigate
clusters.summary: Canadian police are investigating an incident involving gunfire directed at the U.S. Consulate in downtown Toronto on Tuesday morning. Two men are suspected of firing a handgun, but there have been no reported injuries. The investigation is ongoing as authorities work to identify the suspects.

clusters.title: G7 Nations Consider Emergency Oil Reserve Release Amid Price Surge
clusters.summary: In response to rising oil prices linked to the ongoing conflict between the US and Iran, the G7 nations are discussing the release of emergency oil reserves. The International Energy Agency is expected to recommend this action as a measure to stabilize the market and mitigate the impact of the crisis. This potential release underscores the global concern over energy security amid geopolitical tensions.

clusters.title: EU Warns Against Returning to Russian Fossil Fuels
clusters.summary: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen has cautioned that resuming imports of Russian fossil fuels would be a 'strategic blunder' for the European Union. This statement reflects ongoing concerns about energy security and geopolitical implications following Russia's actions in Ukraine. The EU continues to seek alternative energy sources to reduce reliance on Russian energy.

clusters.summary: Jeremy Carl has withdrawn his nomination for a State Department position following bipartisan criticism of his past comments regarding race and Israel. His statements raised concerns among senators, ultimately jeopardizing his confirmation. The withdrawal highlights ongoing tensions surrounding discussions of race and culture in political appointments.

clusters.title: Iranian Footballer Reverses Decision on Australian Asylum Offer
clusters.summary: A member of the Iranian women's football team, who was among seven players granted asylum in Australia, has decided to return to Iran. The player reportedly changed her mind after discussions with her teammates and officials. This development highlights the complexities surrounding asylum decisions and the pressures faced by athletes in such situations.

clusters.title: Trump Predicts Quick End to Iran War and Lower Oil Prices
clusters.summary: President Trump announced that the ongoing conflict in Iran will conclude 'very soon,' suggesting that this resolution could lead to a decrease in oil prices. The implications of the war and its economic impact have raised concerns among voters, who may hold Trump accountable for rising costs. The situation remains fluid as the administration navigates the complexities of the conflict.

clusters.title: Alabama Governor Commutes Death Sentence of Inmate Involved in Murder
clusters.summary: Alabama Governor Kay Ivey has commuted the death sentence of a 75-year-old inmate who was set to be executed for a murder he did not directly commit. The inmate's accomplice fired the fatal shot, raising questions about the fairness of his sentencing. This decision highlights ongoing debates about the death penalty and its application in cases involving multiple participants.

This legal action comes amid increasing scrutiny of AI's role in defense and security sectors.

This incident highlights the vulnerabilities in airline systems and their impact on travel schedules.

clusters.title: Tommy DeCarlo, Lead Singer of Boston, Passes Away at 60
clusters.summary: Tommy DeCarlo, the lead singer of the rock band Boston, has died at the age of 60 after a battle with brain cancer. DeCarlo gained fame after a Myspace tribute led to his joining the band, where he contributed to their legacy in the music industry. His passing marks a significant loss for fans and the rock community.

clusters.title: Mojtaba Khamenei Named Iran's New Supreme Leader
clusters.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei has been appointed as the new supreme leader of Iran, succeeding his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. This leadership transition is significant as it may influence Iran's domestic policies and international relations, particularly amid rising tensions with Saudi Arabia and other regional powers. The appointment has drawn varied reactions, reflecting concerns over continuity in Iran's political landscape.

clusters.title: Bam Adebayo Scores 83 Points, Second-Most in NBA History
clusters.summary: Bam Adebayo of the Miami Heat scored 83 points in a single game, marking the second-highest point total in NBA history, just behind Wilt Chamberlain's legendary 100-point game. This remarkable achievement highlights Adebayo's scoring prowess and contributes to his growing legacy in the league. The performance has sparked discussions about his place among the all-time greats in basketball.

clusters.title: Tornadoes Strike Illinois and Indiana, Causing Fatalities and Damage
clusters.summary: Severe tornadoes swept through parts of Illinois and Indiana, resulting in two fatalities and significant damage to homes and infrastructure. The storms, which affected areas south of Chicago, also caused downed trees and power lines, leading to an overwhelming number of emergency calls. Authorities are assessing the impact as more severe weather is expected in the region.

clusters.summary: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has announced plans to target economic and banking interests linked to the US and Israel in the region. The IRGC released a list of US companies with Israeli ties, claiming their technology has been utilized for military purposes. This declaration underscores ongoing tensions between Iran and these nations and raises concerns about potential repercussions for international business operations.

clusters.summary: US President Donald Trump has expressed that Iran is welcome to compete in the upcoming FIFA World Cup, as stated by FIFA President Gianni Infantino. The decision is significant as it reflects a willingness to engage through global events despite political conflicts.

clusters.title: Pentagon Reports 140 U.S. Troops Wounded in Iran Conflict
clusters.summary: The Pentagon has confirmed that approximately 140 U.S. troops have been wounded in the ongoing conflict with Iran, with eight sustaining severe injuries. Most of the injuries are reported to be minor, and this updated figure marks a significant increase from previous disclosures. The situation underscores the escalating nature of U.S. military involvement in the region.

clusters.title: Judge Rules Against Conservative Media Figures' Press Pass Request
clusters.summary: A federal judge has denied a restraining order sought by three conservative media figures who challenged Washington state lawmakers' decision to withhold press passes. The ruling affirms the lawmakers' authority in determining press credentials, highlighting ongoing tensions regarding media access and representation. clusters.title: Trial Begins for Four Men Charged in Jovenel Moïse Assassination
clusters.summary: The trial of four men accused of assassinating Haitian President Jovenel Moïse in 2021 has commenced in Miami. Prosecutors allege that the motivations behind the assassination were driven by greed, arrogance, and a quest for power. This case highlights ongoing concerns about political violence and instability in Haiti.

items.title: Haiti president’s assassination driven by greed and power, US prosecutors say
items.description:
Opening statements begin in Miami trial of four men accused in the 2021 killing of Jovenel Moïse

  • Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox

Greed, arrogance and power were the driving forces behind four men charged in the US for the 2021 assassination of Haiti’s last elected president, Jovenel Moïse , prosecutors told a court on Tuesday during opening statements.

Federal prosecutors and defense attorneys began presenting opening statements in the trial in Miami for Arcangel Pretel Ortíz, Antonio Intriago, Walter Veintemilla and James Solages. They are charged with conspiring in south Florida to kidnap or kill Haiti’s former leader. Moïse’s assassination led to unprecedented turmoil in the Caribbean nation, where gang leaders have grown increasingly violent and empowered.

Continue reading...

items.title: Trial opens in Miami for 4 men charged in Haitian President Jovenel Moïse's assassination
items.description: Prosecutors say greed, arrogance and power were the driving forces behind four men charged in U.S. federal court for the 2021 assassination of Haitian President Jovenel Moïse
